paper_map.title: Optimization of Process Conditions for Ultralightweight Steel with More Than 13 wt% of Al and 5 wt% of Cr
paper_map.abstract_summary: An ultralightweight Fe–30Mn–13.2Al–1.6C–5Cr steel with >13 wt% Al, reducing density by 20%, was developed and successfully fabricated by optimizing hot rolling and heat treatment. Soaking at 1100 °C before hot rolling avoided intergranular cracking, while higher soaking temperatures (1150, 1200 °C) caused coarse grains and brittle ordered DO3 phase at grain boundaries leading to cracks. After homogenization at 1050 °C for 2 h, water quenching produced a uniform microstructure (austenite, fine κ-carbide, minimal DO3) and excellent tensile properties (1048 MPa UTS, 38% elongation), whereas air cooling resulted in grain boundary DO3 and premature brittle fracture.
paper_map.research_objective: To develop an ultralightweight Fe–Mn–Al–C steel containing more than 13 wt% Al by suppressing brittle phases (coarse κ-carbide, ferrite, ordered DO3) through Cr addition and optimization of hot rolling and heat treatment conditions to achieve successful fabrication and good tensile properties.
main_process_variables: Cooling method after homogenization (air cooling, water quenching)
expected_information_axis: Soaking temperature → microstructure (grain size, DO3 phase fraction and morphology) and hot-rollability
Cooling method → presence of DO3 phase and tensile properties
